[PDF_PAGE 26 | score=5] f I I A Look at Druid Kits This section provides a short explanation of the structure of kit entries. The entries them- selves fpllow in alphabetical order. .’, .a Kit Structure Each kit entry begms with a uescriptiun discussing the nature of the kit and listing any special requirements a character needs to take it. (For instance, to live as a Savage druid, the character must have been born into-or adopted by-a primitive tribe.) This description introduces an archetype character designed to demonstrate general attributes of the kit, not to serve as a character you, the player, have to role-play in a campaign. A character of either gender can take any kit. The kit entries also include the fol
[PDF_PAGE 40 | score=5] Totemic Druid The Totemic Druid closely identifies with a particular species of mammal, reptile, or bird. While Vanier, a typical Totemic Druid, stops short of worshiping his totem animal, he believes that particular animal represents his spirit. The Totemic Druid picks a normal (real-world) wild mammal, reptile, or bird as his totem. This creature cannot be larger than a bear or smaller than a mouse. Some com- mon choices include the black bear, bobcat, eagle, owl, wolf, rattlesnake, and beaver. In addition, Vanier’s totem animal must corre- spond to his branch; if Vanier belongs to the desert druid branch, he can select as his totem only an animal that normally lives in the desert. Role: T
[PDF_PAGE 28 | score=4] court: He suffers a minimum -2 reaction penalty from the lord and court-possibly from all in the region (if his bad advice led to a spectacular failure, like defeat on the battle- field). Depending on the lord’s temper, an Adviser who has fallen into disfavor may face exile or worse until he makes amends. Wealth Options: 3d6x10 gp. Avengbr The venger druid has seen Nature suffer great P rongs. Take the case of the druid Tor- rens. ( illustration.) He had hoped to live as a G P! ardian or Village Druid (listed later in this chppter). However, during his training, forces defiled the area under his protection and sl$w his mentor. [PDF_PAGE 35 | score=4] becomes a good role for an NFC druid Xenia (as either a doddering old sage or a brash young student) hires a party to accompany her on a dangerous scientific expedition to visit a living island spotted in a sahuagin- controlled ocean. A party also might accom- pany her to study the ecology of the salaman- der on the Elemental Plane of Fire or to check out a rumor that a previously extinct species of giant owl now lives in the woods by a lich‘s castle. Branch Restrictions: Arctic and jungle druids cannot take this kit, as their harsh home terrain forces them to devote their time to mere survival, not scientific pursuits. Weapon Proficiencies: Recommended- staff. Secondary Skills: Hunter, navi
[PDF_PAGE 11 | score=3] Wood: 10% cost; 50% weight; -2 damage; -3 to attack roll. The damage modifier reduces the damage normally done by the weapon, with a mini- mum of 1 point of damage. The attack roll modifier does not apply to missile weapons, as the attack roll reflects the character’s aim and is not a function of the material used to make the weapon. Damage modifiers do apply to missile weapons, however. Enchanted nonmetallic weapons must overcome the negative modifiers, too; thus a bone dagger +1 works just as well as a normal steel dagger. Whenever a nonmetallic weapon inflicts maximum damage in combat, it has a 1 in 20 chance of breaking and becoming useless. (The DM rolls a d20.) Nonweapon and Weapon Pro
[PDF_PAGE 13 | score=3] do has @e following granted powers: Redeives a +2 bonus to all saving throw. vs. cold based attacks. Le a, the languages of intelligent mon- sters wpose natural habitats are tundra, arc- tic, an4 subarctic regions. The arctic druid gains o$e extra proficiency slot for this pur- pose ev#ry three levels (at 3rd, 6th, etc.). Such languages include those spoken by ettins, frost gibnts, ice toads, selkies, werebears, .white dkagons, winter wolves, verbeeg, yeti, and oth+rs. Ignbres the effects of freezing weather upon lu+nself at 3rd level. [PDF_PAGE 18 | score=3] jungles or tropical swamps. Spedial Limitation: None. Hal$ Symbol and Grove: The jungle druid uses a kom-tom (jungle drum) as a holy sym- bol. Cbnstructing a replacement takes two weeks, The grove is usually a circle of trees, often ?ear a waterfall. I Mounfain Druid I The mountain druid dwells in areas of ;ugged hills, alpine forests, and peaks and rocks above the tree line. Members of this branch, such as Dansil (illustrated on p. 19), wield over their environments a power gained from the element of earth and espe- cially from stone, They also draw power frolr the weather, especially storms and clouds Dansil~ and his fellows protect mduntains and alpinelflora and fauna from those who would
